purpose: Per-segment patchwise heat ledger for Salt 2/3/4 Jin mainline continuations: wallHeatFlux integral per patch group, BC types, mdot, and energy balance check
scope: Salt 2/3/4 Jin mainline continuation runs (2026-06-18 envelope wave); latest available time step from postProcessing/wallHeatFlux FO output; 5 patch groups (heater, cooler, ambient_wall, test_section, junction_other); 5 spans (lower_leg, downcomer, cooling_branch, upcomer, junction)

key_findings.test_section_note: wallHeatFlux is NEGATIVE (-5.7 to -16.8 W) despite Q=37W BC input; hot upcomer fluid loses heat through quartz to ambient
key_findings.enthalpy_change_W: NaN — HTC CSV provides only mid-span T_bulk, not inlet/outlet per span

open_questions_for_user: Should enthalpy_change_W be computed by extracting T_bulk at NCC interface stations from temperature_probes postProcessing?

Is the test section (pipeleg_left_04) actually intended to heat the fluid, or is it purely an observation tube? The 37W electrical input in the BC does not overcome ambient heat loss at operating temperatures.
Should the net balance check use a time-averaged wallHeatFlux rather than the latest single time step to reduce quasi-steady oscillation noise?
